title: Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series – Touchscreen Calibration Off / Dead Spots
description: Structured troubleshooting guide for touchscreen misalignment or dead spots on a Connex 6000 Series monitor.

steps.title: Check for Obstructions or Contamination
steps.instructions: Inspect the screen for dirt, dried disinfectant residue, moisture, or debris. Clean the screen using an approved disinfectant wipe. Allow the screen to fully dry before testing. Ensure no plastic film, tape, labels, or screen protectors are present.

steps.title: Confirm the Monitor Is Not Frozen
steps.instructions: Press multiple areas of the screen. Attempt to access different menus. Observe whether the display is updating normally or appears stuck. If the screen is frozen but powered on, proceed to a power cycle.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Monitor
steps.instructions: Turn the monitor off. Wait at least 10 seconds. Power the monitor back on. Allow the unit to complete a full boot sequence before testing touch response.

steps.title: Check for Visible Screen Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect for cracks, impact points, pressure marks, or discoloration. Lightly touch various areas and document where input is not detected. Note if touches register in the wrong location (misalignment).

steps.title: Observe and Document the Behavior Pattern
steps.instructions: Determine whether the issue is: Consistent dead zones in specific areas. Random intermittent non-response. Consistent touch offset (pressing one location activates another area). Complete non-response. Document any on-screen error messages or unusual behavior.

CCR.Complaint: “Touchscreen not responding correctly; dead spots on right side of screen.”
CCR.Cause: “Screen cleaned and dried. Unit power cycled. No visible cracks. Touch input remains unresponsive in upper-right quadrant. Suspected touchscreen digitizer failure.”
CCR.Resolution: “Removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for repair.” / “Tagged for Clinical Engineering bench evaluation.”

helpfulDetails: Final disposition (returned to service or sent for repair)

title: Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series – NIBP Failure / Leaky Hose / Transducer Drift
description: Step-by-step guide for Clinical Engineering to troubleshoot NIBP errors caused by hose leaks or transducer drift on the Connex 6000 Series.

steps.title: Inspect the NIBP Hose
steps.instructions: Ensure the hose connecting the cuff to the monitor is fully seated at both ends. Examine the hose for cracks, holes, kinks, or wear. Replace the hose if any visible damage or leaks are detected. Even a small leak can prevent proper inflation and trigger an NIBP error.

steps.title: Check the Cuff and Connections
steps.instructions: Confirm the cuff is the correct size for the patient. Verify the cuff tubing is fully inserted and the hook/Velcro is secure. Inspect the bladder for tears or leaks. A poorly seated or damaged cuff can cause failed or inaccurate readings.

steps.title: Verify the Module / Transducer Seating
steps.instructions: Ensure the NIBP module is fully inserted in the monitor. Check connector pins for cleanliness, corrosion, or damage. Look for visible moisture or debris around the module. Loose or misaligned transducers can cause drift or persistent errors.

steps.title: Observe the Display
steps.instructions: Note any error codes (e.g., “NIBP Fail,” “Low Pressure,” “Leak Detected”). Record whether the error occurs immediately or intermittently. Documenting these details helps Clinical Engineering track recurring issues.

steps.title: Try a Simple Reset
steps.instructions: Remove the cuff and hose from the patient. Power cycle the monitor. Reconnect the hose and cuff, then attempt another measurement. If successful, the issue may have been a temporary connection or seating problem.

steps.title: If the Problem Persists
steps.instructions: If NIBP measurements still fail after completing the above steps, you have already checked the most common and easily verified causes: Hose integrity, Cuff correctness and placement, Module/transducer connections, Simple reset. The issue is now likely internal (transducer drift, module malfunction, or sensor calibration issue) and cannot be resolved at the bedside. Next Steps: Remove the monitor from clinical service. Clearly label it as Out of Service. Send it for repair or bench evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: NIBP measurement failed; error displayed.
CCR.Cause: Hose inspected and intact, cuff verified, module seated correctly. Measurement still fails. Suspected transducer drift or internal module fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed monitor from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for repair. Tagged for bench evaluation by CE.

helpfulDetails: Any visible damage, moisture, or debris
Final device disposition (returned to service or sent for repair)

title: Welch Allyn SureTemp Plus 692 Thermometer - Won’t Power On / Random Shutdowns
description: Guide to troubleshoot power-on failures and random shutdowns in the Welch Allyn SureTemp Plus 692 thermometer, focusing on external causes before assuming internal failure.

steps.title: Check Battery Condition
steps.instructions: Remove the battery from the thermometer. Inspect for corrosion, leakage, or swelling. Replace with a fully charged, compatible battery if needed.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Contacts
steps.instructions: Ensure the contacts inside the battery compartment are clean and free of debris. Lightly clean contacts with a dry cloth or a pencil eraser if corrosion is present.

steps.title: Test with a Known Good Battery
steps.instructions: Insert a new or verified fully charged battery. Power on the thermometer.

steps.title: Check Power Adapter (if using AC/charging)
steps.instructions: Verify the AC adapter is rated correctly for the device. Confirm outlet functionality by testing with another device. Inspect adapter and cable for fraying or damage.

steps.title: Inspect Thermometer Housing
steps.instructions: Check for signs of physical damage or moisture ingress. Ensure the unit is not excessively hot or cold.

steps.title: Reset the Device (if applicable)
steps.instructions: Some SureTemp 692 models allow a power reset by removing batteries for 1–2 minutes. Reinsert batteries and power on.

steps.title: Observe Operation
steps.instructions: Power on and monitor for stability. Note any pattern of shutdowns (immediately after powering on, during measurement, or randomly).

CCR.Complaint: Thermometer will not power on or randomly shuts down during use.
CCR.Cause: Battery contacts were corroded and original battery was weak.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned battery contacts, replaced with a fully charged battery, device powers on consistently.

helpfulDetails: Presence of any corrosion or leakage
AC adapter voltage and outlet tested

title: Welch Allyn SureTemp Plus 692 Thermometer – Inaccurate Temperature Readings
description: Guide for troubleshooting inaccurate readings on Welch Allyn SureTemp Plus 692 thermometers, focusing on external causes before assuming internal failure.

steps.title: Verify Probe Type and Compatibility
steps.instructions: Ensure the correct probe is attached (oral, rectal, or axillary). Incompatible or damaged probes can produce inaccurate readings.

steps.title: Inspect Probe Covers
steps.instructions: Check that probe covers are intact and properly seated. A torn, loose, or reused cover can cause false readings.

steps.title: Check Device Calibration and Recent Verification
steps.instructions: Review the last calibration or verification date. If overdue, follow facility procedure for recalibration.

steps.title: Test on Known Reference Temperature
steps.instructions: Use a calibration check source (e.g., water bath or calibration simulator) to verify accuracy. This helps isolate whether the issue is the thermometer or technique.

steps.title: Check Battery / Power Status
steps.instructions: Low batteries can sometimes cause erratic readings. Replace batteries if low and retest.

steps.title: Examine Probe for Physical Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions: Look for cracks, debris, or dried fluid on the probe tip. Clean per manufacturer instructions; do not immerse in liquid beyond specifications.

steps.title: Ensure Proper Measurement Technique
steps.instructions: Follow correct placement and timing instructions per patient site. Patient movement or improper placement can skew readings.

CCR.Complaint: Thermometer giving readings 1–2°C higher than expected on multiple patients.
CCR.Cause: Probe covers intact; device cleaned; calibration overdue; low battery noted.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced batteries, verified readings against calibration source. Out-of-spec readings persisted; device removed from service and labeled OOS for repair.

helpfulDetails: Temperature readings compared to reference source
Final device status (removed, repaired, returned to service)

title: Welch Allyn SureTemp Plus 692 - Thermometer Won’t Take a Reading / Stuck on 'LO' or 'HI'
description: Guide to troubleshoot a SureTemp Plus 692 thermometer that fails to take readings or displays 'LO'/'HI'.

steps.title: Verify Correct Probe and Probe Cover
steps.instructions:
Ensure you are using a Welch Allyn-approved disposable probe cover on the appropriate probe. Using incorrect or no cover can cause inaccuracies or failed readings.
Confirm the correct probe type (oral/axillary or rectal) is installed for the intended measurement site.

steps.title: Check Probe Seating and Connection
steps.instructions:
Remove the probe from the probe well and then firmly reseat it until it clicks.
Inspect the probe cable and connector for damage, kinks, or corrosion.
A poorly seated or damaged probe often prevents valid readings.

steps.title: Inspect Ambient Conditions
steps.instructions: Make sure the thermometer and probe are within their operating temperature range. If the ambient temperature is too low or too high, the device may display an error instead of reading. Move the device into a normal room environment and allow it to stabilize for a few minutes.

steps.title: Battery Check
steps.instructions: Weak batteries can cause erratic behavior or failure to complete prediction cycles. Replace all three AA batteries with fresh alkaline batteries and retry.

steps.title: Try a New or Known‑Good Probe
steps.instructions: If available, swap in a different Welch Allyn probe and well assembly. Probe thermistors can fail or drift, leading to LO/HI readings; a good probe will isolate whether the probe assembly or the main unit is at fault.

steps.title: Confirm Proper Measurement Technique
steps.instructions:
Ensure the probe is in firm contact with the patient’s tissue and held steadily during the measurement cycle.
If taking axillary temperatures, the arm should be snug against the body to minimize ambient air exposure.

steps.title: Attempt a Cycle Reset
steps.instructions: Eject the probe from the well and then reinsert it. This can sometimes “reset” the measurement cycle and clear transient errors.

steps.title: Check for Device Security States
steps.instructions: Model 692 has security features that can disable operation (e.g., temperature count exhausted) showing messages rather than temperatures. Refer to the manual if SEC or other security indicators appear.

CCR.Complaint: User reports that the SureTemp Plus 692 displays “LO”/“HI” and fails to complete a temperature reading.
CCR.Cause: Verified poor probe contact, out‑of‑range ambient conditions, and low batteries were ruled out; error remained after trying a known‑good probe and fresh batteries.
CCR.Resolution: Unit removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent to authorized service center for repair.

title: Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series – SpO₂ Probe Not Detected
description: Troubleshooting guide for situations where a Connex 6000 Series monitor reports that the SpO₂ probe is not detected.

steps.title: Confirm the Patient Is Not Actively Being Monitored
steps.instructions: Ensure the device is not currently connected to a patient for monitoring. If the device is in use, move the patient to another monitor before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check the SpO₂ Probe Connection
steps.instructions: Inspect the SpO₂ probe connection at the monitor. Make sure the connector is fully seated in the SpO₂ port and not partially disconnected. Loose connectors are a common cause of probe detection errors.

steps.title: Verify the Correct Port Is Being Used
steps.instructions: Confirm that the probe is connected to the correct SpO₂ port on the monitor or SpO₂ module. Some monitors have multiple accessory ports, and incorrect placement may prevent detection.

steps.title: Inspect the Probe Cable and Connector
steps.instructions: Examine the probe cable for visible damage such as cuts, fraying, bent pins, or contamination. Damaged cables can interrupt communication between the probe and the monitor.

steps.title: Swap the SpO₂ Probe with a Known Good Probe
steps.instructions: Disconnect the current probe and connect a known working probe. If the new probe is detected, the original probe is likely faulty. If the probe is still not detected, the issue may involve the cable, module, or monitor port.

steps.title: Check the SpO₂ Extension Cable (If Present)
steps.instructions: Some setups include an extension cable between the monitor and probe. Remove the extension cable and connect the probe directly to the monitor. Extension cables are common failure points.

steps.title: Inspect for Contamination in the Port
steps.instructions: Look inside the SpO₂ port for debris, fluid intrusion, or bent contacts. Contamination can prevent proper electrical contact between the probe and the monitor.

steps.title: Reboot the Monitor
steps.instructions: Power cycle the monitor by turning it off, waiting several seconds, and turning it back on. Temporary software communication issues may clear after a reboot.

steps.title: Verify SpO₂ Module Recognition (If Modular Configuration)
steps.instructions: If the device uses a removable SpO₂ module, confirm that the module is properly seated and recognized by the monitor. A loose module connection may prevent probe detection.

steps.title: Test the Monitor with Multiple Probes
steps.instructions: Testing with two or more known-good probes can help confirm whether the issue is accessory-related or related to the monitor hardware.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reported that the Connex 6000 monitor displays 'SpO₂ probe not detected' and no oxygen saturation values appear on the screen.
CCR.Cause: Damaged SpO₂ probe cable found during inspection. Monitor successfully detected a known-good probe.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced faulty SpO₂ probe with a working unit and verified SpO₂ waveform and numeric values returned to normal operation.

helpfulDetails: Any contamination found in the port
Final operational status of SpO₂ measurement

title: Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series – Temperature Probe Error
description: Troubleshooting a temperature probe error on the Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series vital signs monitor.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Verify the device is not actively being used on a patient. If the monitor is in use, move the patient to another monitor before beginning troubleshooting. This ensures patient care continues without interruption while the device is evaluated.

steps.title: Confirm the error message displayed on the monitor
steps.instructions: Verify the monitor specifically indicates a temperature probe error. This helps confirm the issue is related to the probe or module rather than a general system fault.

steps.title: Inspect the temperature probe connection
steps.instructions: Ensure the probe is fully seated in the temperature port on the side of the monitor. Loose or partially connected probes are a common cause of detection errors.

steps.title: Check for visible damage to the probe and cable
steps.instructions: Look for frayed cables, bent connectors, cracked housings, or exposed wiring. Damaged probes can trigger errors or prevent the monitor from detecting the probe.

steps.title: Disconnect and reconnect the probe
steps.instructions: Remove the probe from the monitor and reconnect it firmly. This can correct intermittent connection issues caused by contact oxidation or incomplete insertion.

steps.title: Verify the correct probe type is being used
steps.instructions: Ensure the probe is compatible with the Connex 6000 Series monitor and matches the temperature module installed (for example, oral/axillary or rectal probe types). Incorrect probes may not be recognized.

steps.title: Check the probe well and connector port
steps.instructions: Inspect the temperature probe well and port for debris, bent pins, fluid contamination, or physical damage that could interfere with the connection.

steps.title: Swap with a known-good temperature probe
steps.instructions: Connect a probe that is known to work properly on another Connex monitor. If the new probe works, the original probe is likely faulty.

steps.title: Power cycle the monitor
steps.instructions: Turn the monitor off, wait approximately 10 seconds, and power it back on. This resets the device and may clear temporary communication faults.

steps.title: Observe monitor behavior during a temperature attempt
steps.instructions: Attempt a temperature reading and watch for any alarms, error codes, or unusual behavior that may indicate whether the issue is probe-related or internal.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reported the Connex 6000 monitor displays a temperature probe error and will not take a temperature reading.
CCR.Cause: Temperature probe cable found damaged near the connector. Monitor displayed probe error when connected.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced faulty temperature probe with known-good probe. Verified temperature measurement functions correctly.

title: Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series – Battery Not Charging
description: Troubleshooting guide for situations where the Connex 6000 Series vital signs monitor battery does not charge.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the monitor is not actively being used on a patient before beginning troubleshooting. If the device cannot maintain battery power, move the patient to another monitor before removing the device from service.

steps.title: Verify the device is connected to AC power
steps.instructions: Confirm the monitor is plugged into a known-good hospital outlet or charging dock. If the device is not receiving AC power, the battery will not charge. Look for power indicator lights, the charging icon on the display, and docking station indicators if used. If the device powers off when unplugged, the battery may be depleted or failed.

steps.title: Check the AC power adapter and power cord
steps.instructions: Inspect the power supply and cord for loose connections, frayed or damaged cable, or bent pins at the connector. If available, test with a known-good power adapter compatible with the Connex 6000 Series. A faulty power adapter is a common cause of charging failure.

steps.title: Inspect the docking station (if used)
steps.instructions: Many Connex 6000 monitors charge through a wall mount or rolling stand dock. Check for loose dock connection, bent or dirty charging contacts, or debris on docking contacts. Remove the monitor from the dock and reconnect it firmly to ensure proper contact. If possible, test charging using the direct AC adapter instead of the dock.

steps.title: Check battery charge status in the system menu
steps.instructions: Navigate to the device settings or battery status screen to confirm battery presence detected, charging status, and battery health information if available. If the monitor does not detect the battery, the battery may be improperly seated or failed.

steps.title: Power cycle the monitor
steps.instructions: A system restart may reset battery management functions. Turn the monitor off, disconnect AC power, wait 30 seconds, reconnect power and restart the device, then observe whether the charging indicator appears after reboot.

steps.title: Inspect battery compartment (external check only)
steps.instructions: If accessible without disassembly, verify the battery is properly installed, ensure the battery latch is secure, and check for corrosion or debris on contacts. If the battery appears swollen, damaged, or hot, remove the device from service immediately.

steps.title: Test with a known-good battery if available
steps.instructions: If a spare battery is available, install a known-good battery, connect the monitor to AC power, and observe whether charging begins. If the replacement battery charges normally, the original battery is likely at end-of-life.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reported the Connex 6000 monitor battery was not charging and the device shut off when unplugged.
CCR.Cause: Battery remained at 0% despite being connected to AC power. Tested with known-good power adapter and dock with same result. Battery likely failed.
CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service and replaced battery. Verified charging indicator and confirmed battery level increased while connected to AC power.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (returned to service or sent for repair)

title: Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series - Touchscreen Unresponsive
description: Guide for troubleshooting an unresponsive touchscreen on the Connex 6000 Series monitor, emphasizing external causes and proper escalation.

steps.title: Confirm the device is powered on and not in sleep mode
steps.instructions: Ensure the monitor is powered on and the display is active. Press the power button once to wake the screen if necessary. A dark or sleeping display can appear unresponsive.

steps.title: Verify the device is not actively monitoring a patient before troubleshooting
steps.instructions: Ensure the device is not in use on a patient. Move the patient to another monitor if necessary before proceeding.

steps.title: Inspect the touchscreen surface
steps.instructions: Check the screen for fluids, gel, dirt, or debris. Dried cleaning solution, ultrasound gel, or bodily fluids can interfere with capacitive touch detection. Clean the screen with an approved hospital disinfectant wipe and dry it with a lint-free cloth.

steps.title: Remove gloves and test touch response
steps.instructions: Some thick or specialty gloves can reduce touchscreen sensitivity. Test the screen briefly with a bare finger to determine if the issue is glove-related.

steps.title: Check for protective covers or screen protectors
steps.instructions: Verify that no aftermarket screen protectors, plastic covers, or tape are interfering with the touchscreen. Remove anything covering the display and test again.

steps.title: Inspect for physical damage
steps.instructions: Check the display for cracks, pressure marks, or liquid intrusion. Physical damage can cause partial or complete touchscreen failure.

steps.title: Reboot the monitor
steps.instructions: Perform a controlled reboot by pressing and holding the power button until the device powers down, then power it back on. Many touchscreen issues are caused by temporary software lockups.

steps.title: Verify system responsiveness during reboot
steps.instructions: Observe whether the screen responds during the boot process. If the display shows normal startup behavior but becomes unresponsive afterward, the issue may be software related.

steps.title: Disconnect external accessories
steps.instructions: Remove USB devices, barcode scanners, or other accessories that may be connected. Occasionally peripheral communication issues can cause interface delays or freezing.

steps.title: Test multiple screen areas
steps.instructions: Tap different areas of the screen to determine whether the issue affects the entire touchscreen or only a specific region. Partial failures often indicate digitizer damage.

steps.title: Check AC power and battery condition
steps.instructions: Ensure the monitor is properly connected to AC power and the battery is not critically low. Low voltage conditions can sometimes cause unstable behavior.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reported the Connex 6000 touchscreen was not responding to touch inputs and they were unable to enter patient information.
CCR.Cause: Touchscreen was completely unresponsive despite normal display operation. Cleaning, accessory removal, and device reboot did not restore functionality.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service and labeled Out of Service. Unit sent to Clinical Engineering shop for display/touchscreen evaluation and potential replacement.

helpfulDetails: Touch response in different screen areas
Final device status (removed from service or returned to use)

title: Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series Vital Signs Monitor - Error: #000000001 Internal Software Error
description: Troubleshooting internal software error #000000001 causing system freezes, reboots, or unusable monitor conditions.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the monitor from active patient use and transition monitoring to a backup device. This prevents loss of monitoring or unreliable readings.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the device and confirm Error #000000001 appears. Observe when it occurs and confirm consistent freeze, reboot, or unresponsive behavior.

steps.title: Perform a Full Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Shut down the monitor, disconnect AC power, remove the battery if accessible, wait 30–60 seconds, then restart. This clears temporary software faults.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Stability
steps.instructions: Verify connection to a known good outlet or battery. Avoid unstable sources. Power instability can cause improper boot sequences.

steps.title: Inspect External Accessories and Modules
steps.instructions: Disconnect all accessories and power on the device. Reconnect one at a time if the error clears to identify faulty components.

steps.title: Check for Environmental or Network Factors
steps.instructions: Disconnect network connections and reboot. Network conflicts can trigger software errors.

steps.title: Attempt System Restart Multiple Times
steps.instructions: Reboot the device multiple times to determine if the issue is persistent or intermittent.

CCR.Complaint: Unit displaying Error #000000001 (internal software error). Device freezing during startup and unable to monitor patients.
CCR.Cause: Internal system/software fault. External power and accessories verified. Error persisted after multiple reboots and isolation steps.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service, labeled out of service, and sent for repair. Backup monitor provided for patient care.

helpfulDetails: When the error occurs (startup, during monitoring, intermittent)
Any alarms, freezes, or reboot behavior observed
Accessories connected or removed during testing
Power source used (AC vs battery) and stability
Final device status (removed from service, sent for repair)

title: Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series Vital Signs Monitor - Error: #000000002 Unclassified Hardware Error
description: Error #000000002 indicating hardware fault causing startup failure, intermittent operation, or loss of monitoring capability.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the monitor from active patient use. Transition to another monitoring device. Expected: Patient monitoring continues safely on alternate equipment. Why it matters: Hardware-related errors can lead to unreliable or failed vital sign readings.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the device and confirm Error #000000002 appears. Observe when it occurs (startup, during measurement, or intermittently). Expected: Error is consistently displayed or reproducible. Why it matters: Confirms this is a true system-reported hardware condition, not a transient glitch.

steps.title: Perform a Full Power Reset
steps.instructions: Shut down the device. Remove AC power and battery (if accessible). Wait 30–60 seconds, then reconnect and restart. Expected: Device boots normally without error. Why it matters: Clears temporary faults and resets internal hardware communication. If resolved → Return to service. If error persists → Continue troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Power Components
steps.instructions: Verify the device is plugged into a known working outlet. Inspect power adapter and cord for damage or looseness. Expected: Stable power with no interruption. Why it matters: Power instability can trigger hardware-related system errors. Try a different outlet and/or known-good power adapter if available.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Condition (if equipped)
steps.instructions: Remove and reseat the battery. Check for swelling, overheating, or poor connection. Expected: Battery seats properly with no visible damage. Why it matters: Faulty batteries can cause internal hardware communication errors. If questionable → Remove battery and operate on AC only, then retest.

steps.title: Disconnect All Accessories and Modules
steps.instructions: Remove all connected accessories (SpO₂ probe, NIBP hose, temperature probe, external modules). Restart the device with nothing connected. Expected: Device powers on without error. Why it matters: A faulty accessory can present as a hardware error. If error clears → Reconnect accessories one at a time to isolate the cause.

steps.title: Inspect Physical Condition of the Device
steps.instructions: Check for signs of damage (drops, cracks, liquid intrusion, debris in ports). Expected: No visible damage or contamination. Why it matters: Physical damage often correlates with internal hardware faults.

steps.title: Verify Software Stability (if accessible)
steps.instructions: Observe for freezing, lag, or abnormal behavior before/after the error appears. Expected: Stable interface and normal operation. Why it matters: Some hardware errors are triggered by failed communication between internal components and software.

CCR.Complaint: Device displaying Error #000000002 (unclassified hardware error), unable to reliably monitor vital signs.
CCR.Cause: External causes ruled out (power source verified, accessories removed, battery checked). Error persists, indicating likely internal hardware failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service, labeled out of service, and sent for repair/bench evaluation.

title: Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series Vital Signs Monitor - Error: #000000003 Graphics RAM POST
description: Troubleshooting Graphics RAM POST failure causing boot and display issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the monitor from patient use before troubleshooting. Patient is safely transferred to another monitoring device. Display failure prevents accurate vital sign monitoring.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the unit and observe startup. Confirm the exact error code (#000000003) appears. Note whether the screen is blank, distorted, frozen, or fails during boot. Confirms a hardware-level self-test failure rather than a transient glitch.

steps.title: Perform a Full Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Shut down the device, disconnect AC power, and remove the battery if accessible. Wait 30–60 seconds, then reconnect and restart. Clears temporary faults or corrupted initialization states.

steps.title: Check External Display Conditions
steps.instructions: Inspect the screen for physical damage, liquid intrusion, and abnormal brightness or flickering. Physical damage can mimic or contribute to graphics-related failures.

steps.title: Verify Power Stability
steps.instructions: Plug the unit into a known working outlet and ensure the power adapter is secure. Observe for consistent startup behavior. Unstable power can cause POST failures.

steps.title: Disconnect External Accessories
steps.instructions: Remove all non-essential accessories and restart the device. External devices can interfere with boot processes.

steps.title: Observe for Heat, Noise, or Additional Fault Indicators
steps.instructions: Check for excessive heat, unusual sounds, or repeated reboot cycles during startup attempts. Helps confirm internal hardware failure.

CCR.Complaint: Unit displays Error #000000003 (Graphics RAM POST) during startup. Monitor does not boot and display is non-functional.
CCR.Cause: Likely internal graphics memory or system board failure indicated by POST error. External power and accessories ruled out.
CCR.Resolution: Performed power cycle, verified power source, inspected display, and removed accessories. Error persisted. Unit removed from service and sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: When the error appears (startup vs intermittent)
Accessories connected or removed during testing
Any unusual sounds or heat observed
Indicator lights or boot sequence behavior
Final device status (removed from service, sent for repair)

title: Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series Vital Signs Monitor - Error: #000000004 System RAM POST
description: Troubleshooting System RAM POST error causing boot failure or startup issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the monitor from patient use if it is unable to boot or function properly. Patient should be transitioned to another monitoring device.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error/Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the device and confirm Error #000000004 appears. Observe for freezing, rebooting, or failure to progress past startup.

steps.title: Perform a Full Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Disconnect AC power and remove the battery. Hold the power button for 10–15 seconds, reconnect power, and restart.

steps.title: Verify Power Source Stability
steps.instructions: Plug into a known working outlet and confirm stable power with proper indicator lights.

steps.title: Remove All External Accessories
steps.instructions: Disconnect all probes, cables, and USB devices. Restart with only power connected.

steps.title: Inspect Physical Condition
steps.instructions: Check for liquid intrusion, damage, loose casing, or signs of overheating.

steps.title: Attempt Multiple Reboots
steps.instructions: Power cycle the unit multiple times to confirm the consistency of the error.

CCR.Complaint: Unit displays Error #000000004 (System RAM POST) during startup and does not complete boot sequence.
CCR.Cause: Internal memory failure suspected after confirming error persists through power cycles, verified power source, and removal of all accessories.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled out of service, and sent for repair/bench evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Alarm or error consistency during startup
Accessories removed or swapped during testing
Indicator lights or boot sequence behavior
Final device status (removed from service, sent for repair)

title: Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series Vital Signs Monitor - Error: #000000005 Watchdog POST
description: Troubleshooting Watchdog POST failure (Error #000000005) causing boot failure, freezing, or restart cycles.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the monitor from patient use immediately. Expected: Patient is transferred to another monitoring device. Why it matters: Device may fail to boot or operate reliably.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error/Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the device and observe startup. Confirm Error #000000005 appears. Note if the unit freezes, reboots, or fails to load. Expected: Error is consistently displayed during power-on self-test (POST). Why it matters: Confirms a system-level failure during initialization rather than a user or accessory issue.

steps.title: Perform a Complete Power Reset
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off, unplug from AC power, and remove the battery (if accessible). Wait 60 seconds, then reconnect and power on. Expected: Device boots normally if error was caused by a temporary fault. Why it matters: Clears transient watchdog or firmware lock conditions. If resolved → Return device to service. If error persists → Continue troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Stability
steps.instructions: Plug the device into a known-good outlet. Avoid power strips or shared circuits. Expected: Stable power during startup with no interruption. Why it matters: Voltage instability can trigger watchdog failures during POST. If power issue found → Correct and retest. If power is stable → Continue.

steps.title: Inspect External Connections and Accessories
steps.instructions: Disconnect all accessories (SpO₂ probes, NIBP hoses, temperature probes, USB devices, modules). Attempt to boot with nothing connected. Expected: Device boots without external interference. Why it matters: Faulty peripherals can interfere with system initialization. If device boots → Reconnect accessories one at a time to identify faulty component. If error persists → Continue.

steps.title: Observe for Environmental or Physical Issues
steps.instructions: Inspect for signs of overheating, liquid intrusion, or physical damage. Ensure vents are unobstructed. Expected: No visible damage or overheating condition. Why it matters: Environmental stress can affect system watchdog behavior.

steps.title: Attempt Alternate Power Method
steps.instructions: If available, attempt to power the unit using battery only or AC only (whichever was not previously used). Expected: Different power source may allow successful boot. Why it matters: Helps isolate internal power regulation vs system board issue.

CCR.Complaint: Unit fails to boot with Error #000000005 (Watchdog POST). Device intermittently restarts and does not reach operating screen.
CCR.Cause: Watchdog POST failure persists after power reset, accessory removal, and power source verification. Likely internal system board or firmware fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed unit from service, labeled out of service, and sent to Biomed/repair vendor for further evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Alarm or error behavior during startup
Power source used (AC vs battery) and stability
Final device status (removed from service, sent for repair)

title: Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series Vital Signs Monitor - Error: #000000006 FLASH Initiation Failed
description: Error #000000006 indicating flash memory initialization failure causing boot failure, startup freezing, or inability to load system software.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the monitor from active patient use. Expected: Patient is transitioned to another monitoring device. Why it matters: A device that cannot complete startup cannot provide reliable monitoring.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error/Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the device and observe the startup sequence. Confirm Error #000000006 appears. Note if the unit fails to boot, freezes, or enters a restart loop. Expected: Error is reproducible during startup. Why it matters: Confirms a true flash initialization failure, not a transient glitch or user-related issue.

steps.title: Perform a Full Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off. Disconnect AC power and remove the battery (if accessible). Wait 60 seconds, then reconnect power and restart. Expected: Device attempts a normal boot sequence. Why it matters: Clears temporary faults that may interrupt flash initialization.

steps.title: Check Power Source Stability
steps.instructions: Plug the unit into a known good, grounded outlet. Avoid power strips or shared circuits. Expected: Stable power during startup. Why it matters: Voltage instability can interfere with initialization of onboard memory.

steps.title: Inspect Battery and Power System
steps.instructions: Check for swollen or damaged battery and loose or intermittent battery connection. Attempt startup with and without the battery installed. Expected: No change in error behavior. Why it matters: Power irregularities during boot can mimic or trigger memory-related faults.

steps.title: Remove External Accessories
steps.instructions: Disconnect all peripherals including NIBP hose, SpO₂ probe, temperature probes, USB devices, and network/Ethernet cables. Restart the unit. Expected: Error persists regardless of accessories. Why it matters: Confirms the issue is internal and not caused by external modules or communication faults.

steps.title: Repeat Startup Attempt
steps.instructions: Perform 2–3 additional restart cycles. Expected: Error consistently reappears. Why it matters: Confirms a persistent failure rather than a one-time boot interruption.

CCR.Complaint: Unit displays Error #000000006 (FLASH initiation failed) during startup and fails to boot.
CCR.Cause: Performed power cycle, verified power source, checked battery, and removed all accessories. Error persisted, indicating likely internal flash memory or mainboard failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service, labeled out of service, and sent to vendor/biomed repair for further evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Alarm or error behavior during startup
Indicator lights or startup sequence behavior
Final device status (removed from service, sent for repair)

title: Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series Vital Signs Monitor - Error: #000000007 A Display System Error
description: Troubleshooting Error #000000007 causing display failure, blank or frozen screen, or unusable interface on the Connex 6000 Series.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the monitor from active patient use or transfer monitoring to another device. Expected: Patient monitoring continues safely on an alternate device. Why it matters: A failed display prevents safe interpretation of patient data.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the unit and confirm Error #000000007 is displayed. Observe screen behavior including blank, dim, frozen, or distorted conditions and when the error occurs. Expected: Error is consistently displayed or display malfunction is clearly present. Why it matters: Confirms a true display/system fault vs temporary glitch.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Stability
steps.instructions: Ensure the device is connected to a known working outlet or docking station. Verify AC indicator if equipped and try a different outlet. Expected: Device powers consistently with stable indicators. Why it matters: Low or unstable power can cause display system errors.

steps.title: Perform a Full Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Shut down the monitor, disconnect AC power, remove battery if accessible, wait 30–60 seconds, then reconnect and restart. Expected: Device boots normally and display initializes correctly. Why it matters: Clears temporary system or display initialization faults.

steps.title: Inspect Display and Physical Condition
steps.instructions: Examine the screen and housing for cracks, pressure damage, liquid intrusion, or misalignment. Expected: Screen is intact with no visible damage. Why it matters: Physical damage often causes display system errors.

steps.title: Check for Overheating or Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Check for excessive heat and blocked vents and ensure proper environmental conditions. Expected: Device is within normal temperature range and well-ventilated. Why it matters: Overheating can disrupt display hardware operation.

steps.title: Attempt Alternate Startup Conditions
steps.instructions: Remove non-essential accessories, undock/redock, and restart without peripherals. Expected: Device boots normally without triggering the error. Why it matters: Helps isolate accessory or docking-related causes.

CCR.Complaint: Unit displaying Error #000000007 with blank/frozen screen; unable to view patient data.
CCR.Cause: Display system error persisted after power cycle, outlet verification, and accessory isolation—likely internal display hardware failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service, labeled out of service, and sent to Biomed/vendor for repair evaluation.

helpfulDetails: When the error appears (startup vs during use)
Accessories or modules connected or removed
Physical condition of screen and housing
Final device status (in service or out of service)

title: Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series Vital Signs Monitor - Error: #000000008 A Real-Time Clock Error
description: Error #000000008 indicating RTC failure causing incorrect date/time, logging issues, or startup errors affecting patient data accuracy.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the monitor from active patient use if time/date accuracy affects documentation or EMR integration. Expected: Patient safely transferred to another monitor. Why it matters: Incorrect timestamps can compromise patient records and clinical decisions.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the device and confirm Error #000000008 appears. Observe if: Time/date resets on power cycle, Device shows incorrect or default time, Error appears at startup or intermittently. Expected: Error is reproducible and timekeeping is incorrect. Why it matters: Confirms a true RTC-related fault versus a transient glitch.

steps.title: Check Date and Time Settings
steps.instructions: Navigate to system settings and verify current date and time. Attempt to manually set the correct values. Expected: Device accepts and retains updated date/time. Why it matters: Misconfiguration can mimic RTC failure. If settings correct and hold → return to service. If settings reset after reboot → continue.

steps.title: Perform a Power Reset
steps.instructions: Power down the unit, unplug from AC power, and remove battery (if accessible). Wait 60 seconds, then restore power and restart. Expected: Device boots normally and retains correct time. Why it matters: Clears temporary faults affecting system clock behavior. If resolved → return to service. If error persists → continue.

steps.title: Check Battery Status (External Check Only)
steps.instructions: Reconnect AC power and ensure the device battery is installed and charging properly (check battery indicator/status). Expected: Battery is recognized and charging normally. Why it matters: Some RTC functions rely on internal backup power stability. If battery not detected or faulty → replace battery and retest. If battery is good → continue.

steps.title: Check for Recent Power Loss or Storage Conditions
steps.instructions: Ask if the device was: Stored without power for an extended period, Fully discharged for a long time. Expected: Normal usage conditions. Why it matters: Extended loss of power can reset RTC and trigger errors. If yes → recharge fully, set time, and retest. If no or issue persists → continue.

steps.title: Inspect for External Signs of Damage
steps.instructions: Check for signs of: Liquid intrusion, Physical impact, Corrosion around battery compartment. Expected: No visible damage. Why it matters: RTC circuitry can be affected by environmental damage. If damage found → remove from service. If no damage → continue.

CCR.Complaint: Unit displaying Error #000000008 (real-time clock error). Time/date not retaining after power cycle.
CCR.Cause: RTC failure suspected after confirming settings reset, power cycle ineffective, and battery functioning properly.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service, labeled out of service, and sent for repair evaluation.

title: Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series Vital Signs Monitor - Error: #000000009 An Audio System Error
description: Troubleshooting guide for Error #000000009 indicating audio system failure affecting alarms and audible alerts.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the monitor from active patient use if alarm audibility is compromised and transfer monitoring to a functioning device.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the device, confirm Error #000000009 is displayed, and test for any audible output such as startup tones or alarms.

steps.title: Check Volume and Alarm Settings
steps.instructions: Verify alarm volume is turned up, not muted, and alarm silence is not active. Adjust settings and retest if needed.

steps.title: Inspect for External Audio Indicators or Accessories
steps.instructions: Check for connected accessories such as nurse call integration or speakers and ensure they are not interfering with audio output.

steps.title: Perform a Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Turn off the monitor, disconnect power and battery, wait 30–60 seconds, then restart to clear temporary faults.

steps.title: Check for Physical Damage or Obstruction
steps.instructions: Inspect speaker openings for debris, fluid ingress, or damage and clean if necessary.

steps.title: Evaluate Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Confirm no exposure to fluid spills, cleaning solution intrusion, or excessive humidity affecting audio components.

steps.title: Functional Isolation
steps.instructions: Determine if the issue is configuration, accessory, or device-related. If unresolved, the issue is internal.

CCR.Complaint: Unit displaying Error #000000009 with no audible alarms or tones during operation.
CCR.Cause: Internal audio system failure suspected after verifying volume settings, performing power cycle, and ruling out external causes.
CCR.Resolution: Removed unit from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for repair evaluation.

helpfulDetails: No audio during startup or alarms

title: Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series Vital Signs Monitor - Error: #000000010 An Ethernet System Error
description: Error #000000010 indicating Ethernet communication failure, resulting in loss of network connectivity, data transmission issues, or inability to interface with EMR systems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Remove the monitor from active patient use if network-dependent functions are required (e.g., EMR charting). Expected: Patient is safely transitioned to alternate monitoring. Why it matters: Loss of connectivity can impact documentation and workflow, though basic vitals may still function locally.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error/Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the device and confirm Error #000000010 appears. Observe when it occurs (startup vs during use). Check if network features (data upload, EMR connection) are unavailable. Expected: Error is present and network functions are impaired or unavailable. Why it matters: Confirms a true Ethernet communication fault, not a temporary disconnect.

steps.title: Check Ethernet Cable Connection
steps.instructions: Inspect the Ethernet cable at both the monitor and wall port. Ensure it is fully seated and not loose. Expected: Cable is firmly connected with no movement. Why it matters: Loose or partially connected cables are a common cause of Ethernet errors. If loose → reseat cable and retest.

steps.title: Inspect Ethernet Cable Condition
steps.instructions: Examine the cable for visible damage (cuts, kinks, bent connectors). Swap with a known working Ethernet cable if available. Expected: Device connects normally with a good cable. Why it matters: Damaged cables can interrupt communication even if they appear connected. If cable replacement resolves issue → return device to service.

steps.title: Check Network Port (Wall Jack)
steps.instructions: Connect another known working device to the same wall port. Expected: Second device successfully connects to the network. Why it matters: Confirms whether the issue is facility network-related vs device-related. If wall port fails → escalate to IT/network team.

steps.title: Verify Network Indicator Lights
steps.instructions: Check for Ethernet port indicator lights (link/activity LEDs) on the monitor. Expected: Lights are active (blinking or solid) when connected. Why it matters: No lights may indicate no physical connection or internal port failure. If no lights → suspect cable, port, or internal Ethernet hardware.

steps.title: Check Network Configuration Settings
steps.instructions: Access device network settings and verify DHCP vs static IP configuration and correct subnet and gateway (if applicable). Expected: Valid IP address assigned and consistent with facility network. Why it matters: Incorrect configuration can trigger Ethernet system errors. If misconfigured → correct settings and retest.

steps.title: Perform Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Shut down the monitor, unplug it for 30–60 seconds, then restart. Expected: Error clears if caused by temporary communication fault. Why it matters: Resets internal network interface and clears transient errors. If resolved → return device to service.

steps.title: Isolate Device vs Network Issue
steps.instructions: Test the monitor on a different known working network port/location. Expected: Device connects successfully on alternate port. Why it matters: Helps determine if issue is environmental or device-specific. Works elsewhere → network infrastructure issue. Fails everywhere → likely internal Ethernet failure.

CCR.Complaint: Device displaying Error #000000010 (Ethernet system error). Unit unable to connect to network or transmit patient data.
CCR.Cause: Ruled out Ethernet cable, wall port, and network configuration. Persistent error indicates likely internal Ethernet hardware failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service, labeled out of service, and sent for repair. Verified alternate monitor available for patient care.

helpfulDetails: Alarm behavior and error timing (startup vs during use)
Final device status (returned to service or sent for repair)

title: Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series Vital Signs Monitor - Error: #000000011 The Touchscreen Controller Failed
description: Error #000000011 indicating touchscreen controller failure causing unresponsive or inaccurate touch input, preventing normal device operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the monitor from active patient use if touchscreen input is required for operation. Expected: Patient is safely transferred to another monitor. Why it matters: Inability to interact with the device can delay or prevent patient assessment.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the device and confirm Error #000000011 appears. Observe touchscreen not responding to input, delayed or inaccurate touch response, and if the error appears at startup or during use. Expected: Error is reproducible and touchscreen functionality is impaired. Why it matters: Confirms a true touchscreen/controller failure rather than user interaction issue.

steps.title: Inspect Screen Surface and Physical Condition
steps.instructions: Check the touchscreen for cracks, scratches, visible damage, fluid contamination, dirt, residue, or protective covers interfering with touch. Clean the screen using approved methods if needed. Expected: Screen is clean, intact, and free of obstruction. Why it matters: Contamination or overlays can mimic touchscreen failure.

steps.title: Perform a Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Turn the device off, unplug from power (if applicable), wait 30–60 seconds, then restart. Expected: Device reboots normally and touchscreen functionality is restored if issue was temporary. Why it matters: Resets the touchscreen controller and clears transient faults.

steps.title: Check for External Input Interference
steps.instructions: Ensure no external USB devices or peripherals are connected and no constant pressure is applied to the screen from objects, mounting brackets, or tight covers. Expected: No external interference affecting touch input. Why it matters: Continuous pressure or interference can cause controller faults.

steps.title: Test Touchscreen Function Across the Display
steps.instructions: Attempt interaction in multiple areas of the screen including menu navigation, buttons at different corners, and varying pressure levels. Expected: Either full failure or consistent dead zones are identified. Why it matters: Helps determine if the issue is localized or controller-wide.

steps.title: Evaluate Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Confirm the device is being used within normal conditions including no excessive moisture, condensation, or extreme temperatures. Expected: Device is in a normal clinical environment. Why it matters: Environmental factors can affect touchscreen performance and electronics.

steps.title: Determine if Issue is Resolved
steps.instructions: If touchscreen responds normally after cleaning, reset, or removing interference, return the device to service.

CCR.Complaint: Unit displaying Error #000000011 with unresponsive touchscreen preventing navigation and vital sign entry.
CCR.Cause: Troubleshooting ruled out contamination, external interference, and power reset; failure consistent with touchscreen controller fault.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled out of service, and sent for repair.

title: Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series Vital Signs Monitor - Error: #000000012 Five or More SMBUS Errors Over a 1-Minute Period Occurred
description: Error #000000012 indicating repeated SMBUS communication failures causing intermittent operation, sensor issues, or system instability.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the monitor from active patient use if vital signs accuracy or device stability is in question. Patient safely transferred to another monitoring device.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error/Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm Error #000000012 is present and observe when it occurs along with any missing parameters, freezing, reboots, or accessory failures.

steps.title: Perform a Power Reset
steps.instructions: Power off, disconnect AC, remove battery if accessible, wait 30–60 seconds, then restart to clear temporary communication faults.

steps.title: Check External Accessories and Modules
steps.instructions: Disconnect all accessories and restart with minimal configuration to identify accessory-related communication issues.

steps.title: Inspect Accessory Ports and Connections
steps.instructions: Check for bent pins, debris, or loose connectors and ensure all connections are secure.

steps.title: Check Power Source Stability
steps.instructions: Verify connection to a stable outlet or charged battery and avoid unstable power sources.

steps.title: Observe for Environmental or Interference Factors
steps.instructions: Evaluate for electromagnetic interference, contamination, or environmental factors affecting communication.

steps.title: Attempt Operation Without Battery (if applicable)
steps.instructions: Run the device on AC power only to determine if the battery is causing communication issues.

CCR.Complaint: Unit displaying Error #000000012 (SMBUS communication errors). Staff reported intermittent readings and occasional system freezing.
CCR.Cause: Internal communication fault suspected after verifying power, removing accessories, and performing reset with no resolution.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled out of service, and sent to Clinical Engineering/vendor for further evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Accessories swapped or removed during testing
Indicator lights or system messages observed
Final device status (in service vs out of service)

title: Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series Vital Signs Monitor - Error: #000000013 The Communications Module or Main Board Failed
description: Error #000000013 indicating communication failure causing loss of module connectivity, network issues, or inability to acquire or transmit patient data.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the monitor from active patient use if communication or data transfer is unreliable. Expected: Patient monitoring safely transferred to another device. Why it matters: Loss of communication can affect documentation and clinical decisions.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the device and confirm Error #000000013 appears. Observe when the error occurs (startup vs during use), if modules (SpO₂, NIBP, temp) are not detected, and if network connectivity or EMR transmission fails. Expected: Error is reproducible with clear loss of communication or module recognition. Why it matters: Confirms a true system-level communication fault.

steps.title: Perform a Full Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Shut down the monitor, disconnect from AC power, and remove the battery (if accessible). Wait 60 seconds, then reconnect and power on. Expected: Device boots normally and error clears if transient. Why it matters: Clears temporary communication or initialization faults. If resolved → Return device to service.

steps.title: Check External Module Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect all connected modules and accessories. Ensure modules are fully seated and latched, disconnect and reconnect each module, and inspect ports for bent pins, debris, or damage. Expected: Modules are recognized after reseating. Why it matters: Loose or poor connections can trigger communication errors. If a specific module is not recognized → Remove it and test device without it.

steps.title: Isolate Accessory or Module Issues
steps.instructions: Remove all external modules and power on the monitor in a minimal configuration, then reconnect one module at a time. Expected: Error does not appear until a faulty module is connected (if accessory-related). Why it matters: Helps determine if the issue is caused by an external module vs internal board. If error returns with a specific module → Replace that module.

steps.title: Check Network / Communication Interfaces (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: If using Ethernet or wireless connectivity, inspect Ethernet cable and port, try a known working cable and port, and verify network link indicators if present. Expected: Stable connection without error recurrence. Why it matters: External communication faults can mimic internal failures.

steps.title: Inspect Physical Condition of Device
steps.instructions: Check for signs of liquid intrusion, physical damage near ports or connectors, and excessive heat or unusual odors. Expected: No visible damage or contamination. Why it matters: Environmental damage can affect internal communication pathways.

CCR.Complaint: Device displaying Error #000000013. Modules not recognized and unable to transmit patient data.
CCR.Cause: Internal communication failure suspected after reseating modules, isolating accessories, and performing power reset with no resolution.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled out of service, and sent for repair. Verified issue not related to external modules or cabling.

title: Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series Vital Signs Monitor - Error: #000000014 Main Board USB Hub Failure
description: Error #000000014 indicating USB hub failure causing loss of peripheral communication and device functionality limitations.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the monitor from active patient use if accessory communication is required for care (e.g., NIBP, SpO₂ modules). Expected: Patient safely transferred to another functioning monitor. Why it matters: Loss of module communication can result in incomplete or missing vital signs.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the device and confirm Error #000000014 is displayed. Observe if the error appears at startup or during use, if accessories are not recognized, and if ports are non-functional. Expected: Error is reproducible and peripherals are not functioning. Why it matters: Confirms a true USB communication failure vs accessory issue.

steps.title: Perform a Full Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Shut down the monitor, disconnect AC power, and remove the battery (if accessible). Wait 30–60 seconds, then restore power and restart. Expected: Device boots normally and error may clear if transient. Why it matters: Resets internal communication buses including USB hub initialization.

steps.title: Check Connected Accessories and USB Devices
steps.instructions: Disconnect all external accessories and restart with no peripherals connected. Expected: Device boots without error if caused by external conflict. Why it matters: A faulty accessory can disrupt the USB hub.

steps.title: Inspect External Ports and Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect USB and module ports for bent pins, debris, or damage. Expected: Ports are clean and intact. Why it matters: Physical damage can mimic internal hub failure.

steps.title: Check for Peripheral Power Indicators
steps.instructions: Reconnect a known-good accessory and observe power and detection. Expected: Accessory powers and is recognized. Why it matters: Helps determine power vs communication failure.

steps.title: Attempt Software Restart / Configuration Reset
steps.instructions: Perform a soft reset or restart system services if available. Expected: Temporary faults may clear. Why it matters: Rules out software-level communication issues.

CCR.Complaint: Unit displaying Error #000000014. Accessories not recognized and unable to obtain vital signs.
CCR.Cause: USB hub communication failure. External accessories and connections ruled out. Issue isolated to internal main board.
CCR.Resolution: Performed power cycle, disconnected all accessories, and inspected ports. Error persisted. Unit removed from service and sent for repair.

title: Welch Allyn Connex 6000 Series Vital Signs Monitor - Error: #000000015 Software Watchdog Timer Reset
description: Troubleshooting guide for watchdog timer reset causing reboots, freezing, or instability.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the monitor from active patient use if it is rebooting, freezing, or unreliable.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm Error #000000015 appears and observe when resets occur and system behavior.

steps.title: Perform a Full Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Shut down, disconnect power and battery, wait 60 seconds, then restart.

steps.title: Check Power Source Stability
steps.instructions: Verify a stable outlet and avoid power strips or shared circuits.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Condition and Connection
steps.instructions: Check battery seating, condition, and test with known-good battery if available.

steps.title: Disconnect External Accessories
steps.instructions: Remove all accessories and restart to isolate potential causes.

steps.title: Check Network Connectivity
steps.instructions: Disconnect network connections and test device standalone.

steps.title: Observe for Overheating or Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Ensure proper ventilation and check for heat or dust buildup.

CCR.Complaint: Device displaying Error #000000015 with intermittent resets during operation.
CCR.Cause: External causes ruled out; watchdog timer resets indicate likely internal system instability or main board/software fault.
CCR.Resolution: Performed power cycle, verified power source, battery, and accessories. Issue persisted. Device removed from service and sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: When the resets occur (startup, during use, intermittent)
Any accessories connected or removed during testing
Power source conditions and battery status
Any error codes or messages displayed
Final device status (removed from service, sent for repair)
